pi_items.source: Package name 'com.google.samples.apps.sunflower' and project structure match official Google Sunflower sample. Web search confirms official repo at https://github.com/android/sunflower. However, local version uses hardcoded plants in PlantRepository instead of Room database + PlantDao like official version.

pi_items.source: task.json description: 'Filter plants by matching against name, description, or grow zone number'. All three properties are available in Plant.kt data class.

privileged_info_summary: The SunflowerApp is a simplified derivative of Google's official Sunflower Android sample app (https://github.com/android/sunflower, archived Aug 2024). The app uses a hardcoded dataset of 5 real plant species in PlantRepository.kt: Apple (zone 3, 30-day watering), Beet (zone 2, 7-day watering), Cilantro (zone 2, 2-day watering), Tomato (zone 9, 4-day watering), and Sunflower (zone 8, 3-day watering). Each Plant object contains: plantId (scientific name-based string), name, detailed description, USDA growZoneNumber (int), wateringInterval (int days), and imageUrl (Wikipedia link). The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SunflowerApp in container execution, sourced from /workspace/data/SunflowerApp. Package structure is com.google.samples.apps.sunflower with MainActivity already defined. The task requires implementing a search feature that filters this plant dataset by name, description, or grow zone number with real-time updates as user types. Data is non-synthetic (based on real plant species with accurate USDA hardiness zones and typical watering requirements).
